title: Potentiometer + LED
subtitle: Control Light with a Knob!
overview: In this session, students learn how a potentiometer works like a volume knob or fan regulator to control the brightness of an LED using Arduino UNO.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — potentiometer wiring, glowing LEDs, Arduino setup, student activities.

gallery.caption: Potentiometer — Adjustable knob to control electricity flow
LED brightness changes with potentiometer rotation

Jumper wires connect all components together

keyTopics: Potentiometer works like a fan regulator or volume knob
LED brightness control using analog values
Arduino reads analog input from potentiometer
Electricity flow control using variable resistance

activity.title: Control LED Brightness using Potentiometer
steps: Place the potentiometer on the breadboard
Connect the other side pin to GND
Connect middle pin to A0 pin of Arduino
Connect LED with resistor to PWM pin 9

exampleCode.title: Arduino Code for Potentiometer + LED

quiz.explanation: A potentiometer is an adjustable resistor used to control current or voltage.

quiz.q: Which real-life device works like a potentiometer?
quiz.explanation: Fan regulators and volume knobs are examples of potentiometers.

quiz.q: Which Arduino pin reads analog values from the potentiometer?
quiz.explanation: Analog pin A0 is used to read potentiometer values.

quiz.q: What happens when you rotate the potentiometer knob?
quiz.explanation: Rotating the potentiometer changes resistance and controls LED brightness.

quiz.q: What is the range of analogRead() values in Arduino?
quiz.explanation: Arduino analogRead() gives values from 0 to 1023.

quiz.q: Which function is used to control LED brightness?
quiz.explanation: analogWrite() controls LED brightness using PWM.
PWM means Pulse Width Modulation — used to control brightness and speed.

quiz.q: Which component glows when electricity flows through it?
quiz.explanation: LED converts electrical energy into light energy.

quiz.q: What is the use of a 220Ω resistor with LED?
quiz.explanation: The resistor limits current and protects the LED.

quiz.q: What does Arduino do in this project?
quiz.explanation: Arduino reads potentiometer values and controls LED brightness accordingly.
